thought_beat_text: He keeps running the reversal: what if the fingerprint in the clay is the point, not the flaw — what if someone left it there on purpose, as a kind of signature, a confession built into the structure. But the argument won't hold its shape; every time he turns it over, the cardboard edge catches the light again and the whole thing goes flat.
residue_summary: The reversal tried to make the seam into sincerity, but the stage light keeps exposing it as construction, and he can't stop seeing both at once.
image_hint: close-up of a thumb pressed into unfired clay, the ridge of the print catching harsh overhead light
audio_hint: low drone with a single dry creak, like a set piece settling under weight
